Class RollElementScene

java.lang.Object
gui.RollElementScene
public class RollElementScene extends Object
The RollElementScene class renders the element rolling screen shown before a battle.

This scene is responsible for:

  • Advancing the stage counter (battle stage progression)
  • Spawning a new monster team using GameEngine.setMonsterTeam()
  • Assigning random elements to monsters and heroes via RandomElementGenerator
  • Allowing the player to re-roll hero elements up to a fixed limit
  • Providing a start button to enter the battle scene

Reroll behavior:

  • At the start of this scene, reroll count is reset to zero
  • The player can reroll hero elements up to GameEngine.getMaxReroll()
  • Rerolling updates the hero element display and the remaining roll counter

UI notes:

  • The left panel shows the roll title and the element effectiveness table
  • The right panel shows monster elements, hero elements, and action buttons

  • Constructor Details

    • RollElementScene

      public RollElementScene()

  • Method Details

    • show

      public static void show(javafx.stage.Stage stage, GameEngine gameEngine)
      Displays the roll element scene on the provided stage.

      This method performs game-state setup, creates UI panels, rolls initial elements, and shows the screen at 1280 by 720 resolution.

      Side effects:

      • Increases stage counter by 1
      • Resets reroll count to 0
      • Sets game state to GameState.ROLL_ELEMENT
      • Spawns a new monster team
      • Assigns random elements to both teams
      Parameters:
      stage - the JavaFX stage where this scene is shown
      gameEngine - the main game engine instance used for navigation and state

    • rollBtnHandler

      private static void rollBtnHandler(javafx.scene.text.Text leftRollText, javafx.scene.layout.HBox heroElementBox)
      Handles the reroll button logic for hero elements.

      This method:

      • Prevents reroll when reroll count has reached the maximum limit
      • Increments reroll count by 1
      • Clears the current hero element UI
      • Assigns new random elements to all heroes
      • Rebuilds the hero element UI overlays
      • Updates the remaining roll text
      Parameters:
      leftRollText - text node showing remaining rolls
      heroElementBox - container holding hero element overlays

    • createButton

      private static javafx.scene.control.Button createButton(String path)
      Creates an image-based JavaFX button with hover glow, hover scale, and press translation animations.

      The returned button is transparent and uses an image as its graphic.

      Parameters:
      path - resource path to the button image
      Returns:
      a configured JavaFX Button
